questions.question: What is negative space in photography?
options: The empty or open area around the main subject
The darkest area of the photo
The part of the image that is overexposed
questions.reviewHint: Negative space is the open, uncluttered area surrounding the subject.

questions.question: If negative space is the area around the subject, what is 'positive space'?
options: The subject itself - the main area of interest
questions.reviewHint: Positive space is the subject; negative space is the area around it.

questions.question: A key benefit of using negative space is that it…
questions.reviewHint: Open space around the subject draws attention to it and isolates it from clutter.

questions.question: Negative space is closely associated with which style of photography?
questions.reviewHint: Minimalist images rely heavily on generous negative space.

questions.question: Negative space can help an image by…
options: Creating a sense of balance and calm
questions.reviewHint: Negative space creates balance, simplicity and often a calm or contemplative mood.

questions.question: Which is a good source of negative space?
options: A clear sky, plain wall or smooth water
A bright distracting highlight behind the subject
questions.reviewHint: Simple, uniform areas like sky, walls or water make effective negative space.

questions.question: Negative space can also be used to…
options: Evoke emotion, such as solitude or scale
questions.reviewHint: Large amounts of empty space can convey isolation, freedom or scale.

questions.question: To make negative space effective, the area around the subject should be…
options: Relatively simple and free of distractions
The brightest part of the frame
questions.reviewHint: Keep the surrounding space clean so it supports rather than competes with the subject.

questions.question: Combining negative space with the rule of thirds often means…
options: Placing the subject off-centre with open space on the opposite side
Placing the subject dead centre every time
Filling the whole frame with the subject
Removing all space around the subject
questions.reviewHint: Off-centre placement plus open space on the other side balances the frame.

questions.question: Light and shadow can contribute to negative space by…
options: Creating clean dark or light areas that surround the subject
questions.reviewHint: Shadow or bright areas can act as effective negative space around the subject.
